1.11a: Address: Sainsbury’s Bank, PO Box 108, Gateway House, Gargrave Road, Skipton, BD23 9EX.
1.7: The lender will not provide finance if the borrower and mortgagor are different parties.
1.14: The firm may act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a partner acts for the lender.
Confirmation must be requested from the address in 1.11a.
1.15: The firm may act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a partner acts for the lender.
Confirmation must be requested from the address in 1.11a.
3.1.3: Seller's legal representative details required only upon specific request.
3.1.4: The seller is required to have legal representation.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents (List A): UK Firearms certificate or shotgun licence, UK Biometric Residence Permits, EU/EEA/Swiss National ID card, Northern Ireland Electoral Identity Card.

Acceptable identity documents (List B): Old style paper UK/NI Driving Licence, HMRC notice of coding (under 12 months old), Council tax demand or statement (under 12 months old), Court appointment instrument (e.g., grant of probate), UK Bank or credit/debit card statement (under 12 months old, excluding internet prints).
3.2.3: Seller's solicitor details required only upon specific request.
3.2.4: The seller must maintain independent legal representation.
4.1: Valuation reports are provided by the lender with instructions unless stated otherwise.
4.3: Report incorrect valuation assumptions to the contact in 1.11a.
4.5a: Re-inspection requests should be directed to the contact in 1.11a.
4.5b: Submit Certificate of Title to the address in 1.11a or via fax to 0345 111 0323.
5.1.1: If the seller owned the property for less than 6 months, contact 1.11a with the previous owner's name/address, previous sale price, and any links between parties.
Certificate of Title must be withheld until lender approval is confirmed.
5.2.1: Contact point 1.11a if the seller is not the registered owner.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
Notify the contact in 1.11a immediately if a Local Authority Search reveals land contamination.
Withhold Certificate of Title until contamination notification is cleared by the lender.
5.4.5: Personal searches are permitted if they comply with Part 1 section 5.4.7.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for remortgages only (excluding unencumbered properties).
Insurance must comply with Part 1 section 5.4.7 and allow for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3a: Report planning breaches or unsatisfied conditions to the contact in 1.11a.
5.5.3b: Original or copies of planning permissions should be held by the borrower.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents should be held by the borrower.
New build flats constructed under regulations preceding the Building (Amendment) Regulations 2018 must be referred to the lender for fire safety review before exchange.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development should be held by the borrower.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting value or marketability to the contact in 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they affect less than 25% of the total property area.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are prohibited unless the borrower owns the entire building freehold, or it is a Tyneside arrangement with 4 or fewer flats.
5.7.1c: No reporting is required if flying freehold or freehold flat requirements in 5.7.1a/b are met.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into 4 or fewer flats are acceptable only if the borrower occupies one flat and owns the freehold.
5.8.5: Reciprocal leasehold/freehold reversion arrangements (crossover leases) are not accepted.
5.9.1: Commonhold is accepted subject to Part 1 section 5.9.2 conditions.
5.10.1: Report use restrictions to the contact in 1.11a.
5.13.1: Report second charges or non-borrower funds for the purchase balance to 1.11a.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 85 years at mortgage start.
New build flats: Minimum 125 years unexpired term.
New build houses: Minimum 250 years unexpired term.
Ground rent for any property type must not exceed 0.1% of the property value.
Ground rent review periods must be greater than 5 years.
Ground rent must not double more frequently than every 20 years.
Ground rent compounded by RPI is unacceptable.
5.14.9: Lease-related matters should be referred to the contact in 1.11a.
5.14.10: Service charge issues should be referred to the contact in 1.11a.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for lease defects is accepted if it covers the specific defect and meets Part 1 paragraph 9 requirements.
Certificate of Title must be withheld until lender approval.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charge receipts should be retained by the borrower and the solicitor.
5.14.13: Notices of service should be retained by the borrower and the solicitor.
5.14.15a: Lending is prohibited if the landlord is absent or insolvent.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is not accepted for cases involving absent or insolvent landlords.
5.15.2a: Report management company problems to the contact in 1.11a.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates should be held by the borrower and the solicitor.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms should be held by the borrower and the solicitor.
5.15.2d: Management company Articles and Memorandum should be held by the borrower and the solicitor.
5.16.2: Contact 1.11a if unable to certify a search entry does not relate to the property.
5.17.5a: Notify 1.11a immediately if Power of Attorney is used.
A certified copy of the Power of Attorney must be provided at completion.
5.17.5b: A statutory declaration of non-revocation for Power of Attorney must be provided at completion.
5.19.1: Lending on affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership is not permitted.
5.20.1: Solar PV lease terms must meet UK Finance minimum requirements; otherwise, the case cannot proceed.
5.20.3: Solar PV leases must include a break clause making the provider responsible for removal costs and damage repairs if the lender terminates the lease.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be reported to the contact in 1.11a.
6.1.3: Notify 1.11a if the borrower does not proceed with the mortgage.
6.4.4: Report all incentives to the contact in 1.11a.
6.4.5: Notify 1.11a if the solicitor does not control all purchase funds.
6.5.1: Vacant possession is mandatory for all transactions.
6.6.1: Refer buy-to-let or letting queries to 1.11a.
6.6.2: Notify 1.11a if letting details are unavailable.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements should be retained by the solicitor and checked for compliance with lender terms.
Agreements should only be sent to the lender if specifically requested.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is not permitted.
6.7.1: Accepted warranties for new/converted properties (last 10 years): NHBC Buildmark, NHBC Solo (pre-2016), Zurich Municipal, Castle 10 (Checkmate), LABC New Home Warranty, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plan Ltd, Buildzone, ICW Residential New Build, The Q Policy.
6.7.2: Warranty documents should be retained by the borrower and the solicitor.
6.7.3: Building standards indemnity assignments should be retained by the borrower and the solicitor.
6.7.4: Properties built/converted in the last 6 years without a warranty are acceptable if they have a Professional Consultants Certificate and evidence of PI cover.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates should be retained by the borrower and the solicitor.
6.8.1: Report lack of agreement/bond for unadopted roads/sewers to 1.11a.
6.10.2: Retentions may be released to either the borrower or the solicitor.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to 1.11a.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options to 1.11a.
6.13.1: Report outstanding improvement or repair grants to 1.11a.
7.3: Consent forms are required for all occupiers aged 17 or over.
Exemption: Occupiers who are children/stepchildren of the borrower aged 17-25 in full-time education.
7.4: Report accuracy doubts regarding disclosed information to 1.11a.
8.1: Third-party advice is permitted if there is no conflict, the document is not open to challenge, and the lender's Independent Legal Advice form is completed.
9.1: The indemnity insurance policy must be sent to the lender after completion.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be the higher of the purchase price or the property value.
10.2a: Funds are disbursed via CHAPS transfer.
10.2b: Minimum 5 working days notice is required for funds.
10.3: Standard deductions include the CHAPS fee and any applicable Product fee.
10.7: If completion is delayed by more than 5 working days, funds must be returned via CHAPS.
Include mortgage account number and Sainsbury’s Bank Reference in the transfer.
10.10: Funds can be held for 5 working days before they must be returned to account 11371266.
10.11: Interest is charged at the mortgage rate from the day funds are released until they are re-credited to the lender.
12.3.1: Contact 1.11a regarding release of retentions or instalments.

14.1.5: The original mortgage deed is sent to HMLR.
